name: Seat 4 Bearings in Carriage Half A

guidance.instructionText: Place all 4 LM8UU linear bearings into carriage half A (y_left_carriage_half_a). Orient each bearing with small ribbed belt hole beside large smooth belt hole.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Flanges prevent axial migration of bearings under load. An inverted bearing will walk out of its pocket within the first few print cycles.

validation.successCriteria: All 4 bearings seated flush. Flanges visible on both sides of each pocket.
validation.failureCriteria: Any bearing recessed, flipped, or able to rock in its pocket.

feedback.successMessage: Bearings seated correctly — ready to close the carriage.
feedback.failureMessage: Re-seat any loose or inverted bearings before closing.

name: Close Carriage Half B — Align Belt Holes

guidance.instructionText: Press carriage half B (y_left_carriage_half_b) firmly against half A, trapping all 4 bearings. Align so the small ribbed belt hole sits beside the large smooth belt hole — this is the ONLY correct orientation.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Belt-hole alignment must be confirmed before bolting. If small-ribbed ≠ beside large-smooth, the peg holes misalign and the belt cannot thread through.

validation.successCriteria: Both halves flush along the full seam. Small ribbed hole is directly beside the large smooth hole.
validation.failureCriteria: Gap visible along seam, or belt holes on wrong sides relative to each other.

feedback.successMessage: Halves closed and belt holes aligned.
feedback.failureMessage: Flip half B 180° and retry — small ribbed hole must be adjacent to the large smooth hole.

guidance.instructionText: Hold both halves compressed firmly by hand — do not bolt yet. Shake the closed assembly firmly for 2–3 seconds. Listen and feel for any bearing rattle or movement.
guidance.whyItMattersText: A rattle means at least one bearing is not fully seated in its pocket. Bolting before fixing locks in the defect — the carriage will develop play under load and the axis will lose positional accuracy.

validation.successCriteria: No audible rattle, no felt movement from bearings while halves are hand-compressed.
validation.failureCriteria: Any rattle, clicking, or loose feeling during the shake.

feedback.successMessage: Shake test passed — bearings are solid.
feedback.failureMessage: Open the halves and identify the loose bearing. Re-seat it and retest before proceeding.

name: Rod Slide Test — Bearing Alignment Check

guidance.instructionText: While still holding halves compressed, slide a smooth 8mm rod vertically through all 4 bearings. It should pass with slight, even resistance — not too stiff, not freely loose.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Uneven resistance exposes bearing misalignment. If left uncorrected, the axis will bind under motor load and cause missed steps or stalls.

validation.successCriteria: Rod slides through all 4 bearings with slight, consistent resistance. No binding or tight spots.
validation.failureCriteria: Rod catches or sticks at any bearing, OR drops through with zero resistance.

feedback.successMessage: Rod slide test passed — bearings aligned.
feedback.failureMessage: Open halves. Check that all bearings are fully seated and coplanar. Re-close and retest.

name: Insert Bolts and Thread 4 Nuts Finger-Tight

guidance.instructionText: Insert the 2 shorter M6×18 bolts through the TOP holes and the 2 longer M6×30 bolts through the BOTTOM holes. Thread all 4 M6 nuts finger-tight — hand only, no drill yet.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Short bolts top, long bolts bottom — this is the load-distribution pattern. Swapping them puts longer threads in the high-stress joint, which can strip faster under Y-axis acceleration.

validation.successCriteria: All 4 bolts inserted and 4 nuts threaded finger-tight. Short M6×18 bolts are in the top holes, long M6×30 in the bottom holes.
validation.failureCriteria: Wrong bolt length in wrong hole, any nut cross-threaded, or any bolt not yet threaded.

feedback.successMessage: Bolts and nuts placed correctly — ready to tighten.
feedback.failureMessage: Verify bolt lengths: M6×18 (shorter) → top holes, M6×30 (longer) → bottom holes.

guidance.instructionText: Using the power drill at lowest torque setting, tighten all 4 carriage bolts in a cross pattern: top-left → bottom-right → top-right → bottom-left. Repeat the pattern once to equalize clamping.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Cross-pattern tightening ensures even clamping force across the carriage body. Sequential (one side first) tightening distorts the bearing bores and causes axis binding.

validation.successCriteria: All 4 bolts tight at lowest torque. Carriage halves fully closed — no gap along seam. Rod still slides smoothly through bearings after tightening. Y-Left carriage complete — 1 of 4
validation.failureCriteria: Any bolt loose, gap visible between halves, or rod now binds after tightening.

feedback.failureMessage: If rod now binds: back off all bolts ¼ turn, re-run cross pattern at lower torque. If gap persists: check for bearing oversize or debris between halves.
